document_metadata.title: Global Industry Classification Standard (GICS) Methodology
document_metadata.subtitle: Guiding Principles and Methodology for GICS

new_rules.name: gics_equity_issuer_eligibility
new_rules.purpose: Restrict GICS classification to equity-issuing companies and exclude non-corporate entity types.

source.markdown: Companies that have issued equity securities are eligible for a GICS classification. If a company's subsidiary files separate financials to its reporting government agency, that subsidiary is considered a separate entity and classified independently under the GICS Methodology. GICS is not assigned to supranationals, municipals, sovereigns, shell companies, mutual funds, or exchange traded funds.
source.section: Section 2: Companies Eligible for GICS Classification

business: No supranational, municipal, sovereign, shell company, mutual fund, or ETF survives filter: offending entity_type={entity_type}
All surviving rows have is_equity_issuer == True

new_rules.name: classify_tracking_stock_by_underlying
new_rules.purpose: Classify a tracking stock based on the underlying business it tracks rather than the issuing parent company.

source.markdown: A tracking stock issued by a parent company is classified based on its underlying business.
source.section: Section 3.2: Propagation of GICS from company level to security level

technical: underlying_business_gics_sub_industry_code non-null for all rows where is_tracking_stock == True
business: Every row with is_tracking_stock == True has gics_sub_industry_code equal to underlying_business_gics_sub_industry_code

new_rules.name: classify_by_primary_revenue
new_rules.purpose: Assign a company to the Sub-Industry whose definition most accurately reflects the activity generating more than 60% of total revenues.

source.markdown: A company is initially classified into the GICS Sub-Industry whose definition most accurately reflects the business activity that generates more than 60% of the company's total revenues.
source.section: Section 3.1: Classification by revenue and earnings

business: All rows assigned via this rule have primary_activity_revenue_pct > 0.60: min observed={min_pct}

new_rules.name: classify_by_majority_revenue_and_earnings
new_rules.purpose: When no single activity exceeds the 60% revenue threshold, assign the Sub-Industry that accounts for at least 50% of both revenues and earnings.

source.markdown: If a company is engaged in two or more distinct business activities and no single activity contributes 60% or more of total revenues, the company is initially classified in the Sub-Industry that accounts for the largest share (at least 50%) of both revenues and earnings.
source.section: Section 3.1: Classification by revenue and earnings

technical: primary_activity_revenue_pct and primary_activity_earnings_pct present and in [0, 1]
business: All rows assigned via this rule have primary_activity_revenue_pct >= 0.50 and primary_activity_earnings_pct >= 0.50

new_rules.name: classify_by_largest_combined_contribution
new_rules.purpose: When no single activity reaches 50% of both revenues and earnings, assign the Sub-Industry representing the largest combined contribution to revenues and earnings.

source.markdown: Where no single business activity represents at least 50% of both revenues and earnings, the company is initially classified in the Sub-Industry representing the activity that provides the largest combined contribution to revenues and earnings.
source.section: Section 3.1: Classification by revenue and earnings

business: Assigned Sub-Industry corresponds to activity with highest sum of revenue_pct + earnings_pct across all candidate activities

new_rules.name: classify_by_qualitative_research
new_rules.purpose: When none of the quantitative revenue or earnings thresholds are met, determine GICS classification through additional research and analysis.

source.markdown: If none of the above quantitative thresholds are met, the company's initial classification is determined based on additional research and analysis.
source.section: Section 3.1: Classification by revenue and earnings

business: All rows assigned via this rule have qualitative_classification_flag == True
gics_sub_industry_code non-null after qualitative assignment

new_rules.name: classify_diversified_conglomerate
new_rules.purpose: Assign companies significantly diversified across three or more sectors with no majority activity to Industrial Conglomerates or Multi-Sector Holdings.

source.markdown: Companies that are significantly diversified across three or more Sectors, with no single Sector contributing a majority of revenues or earnings, are classified either in the Industrial Conglomerates Sub-Industry (within the Industrial Sector) or in the Multi-Sector Holdings Sub-Industry (within the Financials Sector).
source.section: Section 3.1: Classification by revenue and earnings

business: All rows assigned via this rule have num_distinct_active_sectors >= 3: min observed={min_sectors}
Assigned gics_sub_industry_code is 20105010 (Industrial Conglomerates) or 40201030 (Multi-Sector Holdings)

new_rules.name: restrict_reclassification_to_sustained_60pct
new_rules.purpose: Limit GICS Sub-Industry changes to cases where a different business activity accounts for at least 60% of revenues over a sustained period, preventing unnecessary turnover.

source.markdown: To ensure consistency and limit unnecessary turnover, changes to a company's GICS Sub-Industry are generally considered only when a different business activity accounts for at least 60% of total revenues over a sustained period.
source.section: Section 3.1: Classification by revenue and earnings

business: No reclassification applied where new activity revenue_pct < 0.60: offending pct={pct}

new_rules.name: minimize_reclassification_on_temporary_fluctuations
new_rules.purpose: Preserve existing GICS classification during review by disregarding temporary fluctuations in results of a company's different activities.

source.markdown: In order to provide a stable Sub-Industry classification, when reviewing a company's classification, changes will be minimized in the Sub-Industry classification to the extent possible by disregarding temporary fluctuations in the results of a company's different activities.

business: No classification change applied where revenue_trend_flag indicates temporary fluctuation

new_rules.name: propagate_gics_from_company_to_equity_securities
new_rules.purpose: Assign the company's GICS classification to all its equity securities, including ADRs and GDRs, so all securities from the same company share a single classification.

source.markdown: GICS is assigned at the company level. Thus, all equity securities including ADRs and GDRs issued by a company have the same classification as the company.
source.section: Section 3.2: Propagation of GICS from company level to security level

technical: company_id and gics_sub_industry_code columns present and non-null
business: All securities sharing the same company_id have identical gics_sub_industry_code: first offending company_id={cid}

new_rules.name: trigger_review_on_restructuring_or_new_annual_report
new_rules.purpose: Initiate a GICS Sub-Industry review when a significant corporate restructuring occurs, a new annual report is published, or a client request is received.

source.markdown: A company's Sub-Industry classification will be reviewed either when a significant corporate restructuring occurs or when a new annual report is available. GICS reviews also occur upon client request.

technical: has_significant_restructuring, has_new_annual_report, client_review_requested all boolean and non-null
business: Review is triggered for every row where any of the three trigger flags is True

new_rules.name: gics_fixed_income_corporate_eligibility
new_rules.purpose: Restrict fixed income GICS classification to corporate bond issuers; exclude supranationals, municipals, and sovereigns from fixed income coverage.

source.markdown: Corporates that have issued bonds are eligible for a classification. Supranationals, Municipals and Sovereigns that have issued fixed income securities are currently not covered under this Methodology.
source.section: Section 8.1: Fixed income universe eligible for classification

business: No supranational, municipal, or sovereign survives FI eligibility filter: offending fi_entity_type={fi_entity_type}
All surviving rows have has_issued_corporate_bonds == True

new_rules.name: propagate_gics_equity_to_corporate_bonds
new_rules.purpose: Assign a company's existing equity GICS classification to its corporate bonds when the company has also issued equity securities.

source.markdown: GICS is assigned at the company level and thus all securities, equities or corporate bonds, issued by the company will have the same GICS classification as the company. MSCI leverages GICS assigned to a company that has issued equity securities and assigns the same GICS classification to the corporate bonds issued by the company.
source.section: Section 8.1.1: Classification of a company that has issued both equity and corporate bonds

technical: company_id, has_issued_equity, gics_sub_industry_code all present and non-null where has_issued_equity == True
business: All corporate bonds of a company where has_issued_equity == True share the company's equity gics_sub_industry_code: first offending company_id={cid}

new_rules.name: classify_bond_only_issuer_by_parent_gics
new_rules.purpose: Assign a bond-only issuer's GICS based on its parent or ultimate parent company's GICS classification as available in GICS Direct.

source.markdown: Each corporate bond issuer is assigned to a GICS Sub-Industry based on the GICS of its related listed entity, i.e., its parent company or ultimate parent company, as available in GICS Direct.
source.section: Section 8.1.2: Classification of a company that has issued only corporate bonds

technical: parent_gics_sub_industry_code non-null for rows where this rule applies
business: All bond-only issuers with available parent GICS have gics_sub_industry_code == parent_gics_sub_industry_code

new_rules.name: classify_bond_only_issuer_without_parent_gics
new_rules.purpose: When no parent GICS is available in GICS Direct, classify the bond-only issuer based on its own or related entities' underlying business using the GICS framework.

source.markdown: In cases where the related entities, i.e., its parent and ultimate parent company don't have GICS in GICS Direct, the corporate bond issuer will be classified based on its own or its related entities' underlying business using the GICS framework.
source.section: Section 8.1.2: Classification of a company that has issued only corporate bonds

technical: parent_gics_sub_industry_code is null for all rows where this rule applies
business: gics_sub_industry_code non-null after fallback classification for all bond-only issuers without parent GICS

new_rules.name: classify_spv_by_related_entity
new_rules.purpose: Assign GICS to a special purpose vehicle or captive finance vehicle based on the business of its related entities rather than its own minimal operations.

source.markdown: if the corporate bond issuer is a special purpose vehicle or a captive finance vehicle, it will be classified based on the business of its related entities as described in Section 3.
source.section: Section 8.1.2: Classification of a company that has issued only corporate bonds

business: All SPV/captive finance rows have gics_sub_industry_code == related_entity_gics_sub_industry_code: first offending row={row}

new_rules.name: review_fixed_income_gics_annually
new_rules.purpose: Ensure fixed income GICS classifications are reviewed at minimum annually and also upon significant corporate restructuring or client request.

source.markdown: A company's Sub-Industry classification will be reviewed at least annually. It will also be reviewed when a significant corporate restructuring occurs. GICS reviews could also occur upon client request.

technical: fi_last_review_date is a valid date and non-null
business: No row has fi_last_review_date more than 12 months before the current review date: offending company_id={cid}
Review triggered for all rows where has_significant_restructuring == True or client_review_requested == True

new_rules.name: apply_structure_review_to_fixed_income
new_rules.purpose: Apply all GICS Sub-Industry definition changes resulting from a GICS Structure Review to fixed income company classifications without exception.

source.markdown: All changes in the GICS Structure and definitions resulting from a Structure Review will be applicable to the classification of companies that have issued fixed income securities as well.
source.section: Section 8.3.2: Impact of GICS structure review

business: All rows where structure_review_change_flag == True and has_issued_corporate_bonds == True have an updated gics_sub_industry_code reflecting the new structure

intra_review_invariants.quote: To ensure consistency and limit unnecessary turnover, changes to a company's GICS Sub-Industry are generally considered only when a different business activity accounts for at least 60% of total revenues over a sustained period.
intra_review_invariants.concern: Between reviews, reclassification is suppressed unless the 60% sustained threshold is met. No mid-period downgrade or reassignment on a single year's result.

intra_review_invariants.quote: In order to provide a stable Sub-Industry classification, when reviewing a company's classification, changes will be minimized in the Sub-Industry classification to the extent possible by disregarding temporary fluctuations in the results of a company's different activities.
intra_review_invariants.concern: During a review, temporary fluctuations in activity-level results must not drive a Sub-Industry change; analyst judgement distinguishes temporary from sustained shifts.

mandatory_fields.name: primary_activity_revenue_pct
mandatory_fields.description: Revenue fraction of the single largest business activity as a proportion of total company revenues

mandatory_fields.name: primary_activity_earnings_pct
mandatory_fields.description: Earnings fraction of the single largest business activity as a proportion of total company earnings

mandatory_fields.name: num_distinct_active_sectors
mandatory_fields.description: Count of GICS Sectors in which the company has material business activity

mandatory_fields.name: entity_type
mandatory_fields.description: Category of the entity: corporate, supranational, municipal, sovereign, shell_company, mutual_fund, or etf

mandatory_products: Broker reports and published research literature (secondary)
GICS Direct (parent company GICS lookup for bond-only issuers)

preprocessing.step: Obtain revenue and earnings breakdown by business activity from annual report
Determine entity type and eligibility flags prior to classification
Look up parent/ultimate parent GICS in GICS Direct for bond-only issuers

mock_datapoints.name: entity_type
mock_datapoints.distribution: categorical with values: corporate (0.80), supranational (0.03), municipal (0.03), sovereign (0.05), shell_company (0.03), mutual_fund (0.03), etf (0.03)
mock_datapoints.description: Legal and structural category of the entity for GICS eligibility determination.
mock_datapoints.source_section: Section 2: Companies Eligible for GICS Classification
definition_notes: Subsidiaries that file separate financials to a reporting government agency are treated as separate entities and classified independently.

mock_datapoints.name: is_equity_issuer
mock_datapoints.description: True if the entity has issued equity securities; required for equity GICS eligibility.
mock_datapoints.source_section: Section 2: Companies Eligible for GICS Classification

mock_datapoints.name: is_subsidiary_with_separate_financials
mock_datapoints.description: True if the entity is a subsidiary that files separate financials to a government reporting agency and is thus treated as an independent classification entity.
mock_datapoints.source_section: Section 2: Companies Eligible for GICS Classification

mock_datapoints.name: primary_activity_revenue_pct
mock_datapoints.description: Revenue fraction of the company's single largest business activity as a share of total revenues, used to determine the 60% and 50% classification thresholds.
mock_datapoints.source_section: Section 3.1: Classification by revenue and earnings
definition_notes: Revenue is preferred over earnings as the primary classification metric because industrial and geographic breakdowns are more commonly available.
Market perception may supplement the revenue and earnings analysis where relevant.
mock_datapoints.semantic_threshold: more than 60% of the company's total revenues

mock_datapoints.name: primary_activity_earnings_pct
mock_datapoints.description: Earnings fraction of the company's single largest business activity as a share of total earnings, used as secondary criterion when revenue alone does not reach 60%.
mock_datapoints.source_section: Section 3.1: Classification by revenue and earnings
definition_notes: Company valuations are more closely related to earnings than revenues; earnings remain an important secondary consideration.
mock_datapoints.semantic_threshold: at least 50% of both revenues and earnings

mock_datapoints.name: num_distinct_active_sectors
mock_datapoints.description: Count of distinct GICS Sectors in which the company has material business activity; used to identify diversified conglomerates.
mock_datapoints.source_section: Section 3.1: Classification by revenue and earnings
definition_notes: Companies with material activity in 3 or more sectors and no single sector majority are candidates for Industrial Conglomerates or Multi-Sector Holdings.
mock_datapoints.semantic_threshold: three or more Sectors, with no single Sector contributing a majority of revenues or earnings

mock_datapoints.name: qualitative_classification_flag
mock_datapoints.description: True when none of the quantitative revenue or earnings thresholds are met and classification requires additional research and analysis.
mock_datapoints.source_section: Section 3.1: Classification by revenue and earnings

mock_datapoints.name: is_tracking_stock
mock_datapoints.description: True if the security is a tracking stock issued by a parent company; such securities are classified based on their underlying business, not the parent's overall GICS.
mock_datapoints.source_section: Section 3.2: Propagation of GICS from company level to security level

mock_datapoints.name: underlying_business_gics_sub_industry_code
mock_datapoints.distribution: discrete uniform over the 163 valid 8-digit GICS sub-industry codes
mock_datapoints.description: The 8-digit GICS Sub-Industry code reflecting the underlying business activity tracked by a tracking stock.
mock_datapoints.source_section: Section 3.2: Propagation of GICS from company level to security level
mock_datapoints.taxonomy: GICS 8-digit hierarchical code (Sector 2-digit, Industry Group 4-digit, Industry 6-digit, Sub-Industry 8-digit)

mock_datapoints.name: gics_sub_industry_code
mock_datapoints.distribution: discrete uniform over the 163 valid 8-digit GICS sub-industry codes
mock_datapoints.description: The current 8-digit GICS Sub-Industry code assigned to the company or security.
mock_datapoints.source_section: Section 1.1: Overview of GICS
mock_datapoints.taxonomy: GICS 8-digit hierarchical code (Sector 2-digit, Industry Group 4-digit, Industry 6-digit, Sub-Industry 8-digit)
definition_notes: The full GICS classification is an 8-digit code with text description.
The hierarchical design allows easy transition between GICS tiers.

mock_datapoints.name: has_significant_restructuring
mock_datapoints.description: True when the company has undergone a significant corporate restructuring, triggering a GICS classification review.
mock_datapoints.source_section: Section 4: Review of GICS Classification
definition_notes: The term 'significant' is not quantitatively defined in the source; determination is qualitative.

mock_datapoints.name: has_new_annual_report
mock_datapoints.description: True when a new annual report has become available, triggering a periodic GICS classification review.
mock_datapoints.source_section: Section 4: Review of GICS Classification

mock_datapoints.name: revenue_trend_flag
mock_datapoints.description: True when the observed change in revenue by activity is judged to be a temporary fluctuation rather than a sustained structural shift, suppressing reclassification.
mock_datapoints.source_section: Section 4: Review of GICS Classification
definition_notes: No quantitative definition of 'temporary' is given; determination is analyst judgement.
mock_datapoints.semantic_threshold: temporary fluctuations in the results of a company's different activities

mock_datapoints.name: fi_entity_type
mock_datapoints.distribution: categorical with values: corporate (0.90), supranational (0.04), municipal (0.03), sovereign (0.03)
mock_datapoints.description: Entity type classification for fixed income GICS eligibility, distinguishing corporate bond issuers from excluded entity types.
mock_datapoints.source_section: Section 8.1: Fixed income universe eligible for classification

mock_datapoints.name: has_issued_corporate_bonds
mock_datapoints.description: True if the entity has issued corporate bonds, making it eligible for fixed income GICS classification.
mock_datapoints.source_section: Section 8.1: Fixed income universe eligible for classification

mock_datapoints.name: has_issued_equity
mock_datapoints.description: True if the corporate bond issuer has also issued equity securities, enabling direct equity GICS propagation to its bonds.
mock_datapoints.source_section: Section 8.1.1: Classification of a company that has issued both equity and corporate bonds

mock_datapoints.name: parent_gics_sub_industry_code
mock_datapoints.distribution: discrete uniform over the 163 valid 8-digit GICS sub-industry codes, with 15% null (no parent GICS available in GICS Direct)
mock_datapoints.description: The 8-digit GICS Sub-Industry code of the bond issuer's parent or ultimate parent company as recorded in GICS Direct.
mock_datapoints.source_section: Section 8.1.2: Classification of a company that has issued only corporate bonds
mock_datapoints.taxonomy: GICS 8-digit hierarchical code (Sector 2-digit, Industry Group 4-digit, Industry 6-digit, Sub-Industry 8-digit)
definition_notes: Null when parent and ultimate parent do not have a GICS in GICS Direct; triggers fallback classification.

mock_datapoints.name: is_spv_or_captive_finance
mock_datapoints.description: True if the corporate bond issuer is a special purpose vehicle or captive finance vehicle; classification is based on related entities' business.
mock_datapoints.source_section: Section 8.1.2: Classification of a company that has issued only corporate bonds

mock_datapoints.name: related_entity_gics_sub_industry_code
mock_datapoints.distribution: discrete uniform over the 163 valid 8-digit GICS sub-industry codes
mock_datapoints.description: The 8-digit GICS Sub-Industry code of the related entity whose business drives the SPV or captive finance vehicle's classification.
mock_datapoints.source_section: Section 8.1.2: Classification of a company that has issued only corporate bonds
mock_datapoints.taxonomy: GICS 8-digit hierarchical code (Sector 2-digit, Industry Group 4-digit, Industry 6-digit, Sub-Industry 8-digit)

mock_datapoints.name: fi_last_review_date
mock_datapoints.distribution: Uniform date in the 12 months prior to simulation date
mock_datapoints.description: Date of the most recent GICS classification review for companies with fixed income securities; used to enforce the annual minimum review cadence.
mock_datapoints.source_section: Section 8.3.1: Ongoing review

mock_datapoints.name: structure_review_change_flag
mock_datapoints.description: True when the most recent GICS Structure Review introduced a change to the Sub-Industry definition applicable to this company, requiring reclassification of its fixed income securities.
mock_datapoints.source_section: Section 8.3.2: Impact of GICS structure review

mock_datapoints.name: client_review_requested
mock_datapoints.description: True when a client has formally requested a review of the company's GICS classification.
mock_datapoints.source_section: Section 4: Review of GICS Classification

final_asserts: Every surviving row has a non-null gics_sub_industry_code: null count={null_count}
gics_sub_industry_code is an 8-digit integer within the set of 163 defined GICS Sub-Industry codes for all rows: first offending code={code}
No row has entity_type in {supranational, municipal, sovereign, shell_company, mutual_fund, etf}: offending entity_type={entity_type}
All equity securities sharing the same company_id have identical gics_sub_industry_code: first offending company_id={cid}
All is_tracking_stock == True rows have gics_sub_industry_code == underlying_business_gics_sub_industry_code: first offending security_id={sid}
All rows with num_distinct_active_sectors >= 3 and no sector majority have gics_sub_industry_code in {20105010, 40201030}: offending code={code}

clarifications_needed.quote: If none of the above quantitative thresholds are met, the company's initial classification is determined based on additional research and analysis.
clarifications_needed.concern: No criteria are specified for what research and analysis qualifies, how conclusions are reached, or who makes the determination. The rule classify_by_qualitative_research captures this step but cannot be made algorithmic without further specification.

clarifications_needed.quote: Market perception may be considered in the classification process where it is relevant to determining a company's appropriate GICS assignment.
clarifications_needed.concern: Market perception is not defined. No criteria specify when it is considered relevant, what weight it carries relative to revenue and earnings thresholds, or how it overrides a quantitative result. A rule cannot be written without this definition.

clarifications_needed.quote: changes to a company's GICS Sub-Industry are generally considered only when a different business activity accounts for at least 60% of total revenues over a sustained period.
clarifications_needed.concern: The duration that constitutes a sustained period is not defined. The rule restrict_reclassification_to_sustained_60pct captures the 60% threshold but cannot enforce the time dimension without a specific period (e.g., two consecutive fiscal years).

clarifications_needed.quote: A company's Sub-Industry classification will be reviewed either when a significant corporate restructuring occurs
clarifications_needed.concern: Significant corporate restructuring is not quantitatively defined. The rule trigger_review_on_restructuring_or_new_annual_report captures this trigger but the significance threshold requires further specification.

clarifications_needed.quote: GLOBAL INDUSTRY CLASSIFICATION STANDARD (GICS) METHODOLOGY APRIL 2026
clarifications_needed.concern: The document cover page consistently states April 2026, but the source PDF filename contains the date 20250220 (February 20, 2025). The authoritative publication date cannot be determined from the source alone. version_date is set to 2026-04-01 based on the cover page.

scoped_out.quote: To provide the level of precision critical in the investment process, the GICS is designed with four levels of classifications that includes 11 Sectors, 25 Industry Groups, 74 Industries, and 163 Sub-Industries.
scoped_out.concern: Section 1.1 and 1.2 enumerate the GICS taxonomy structure (sector codes, hierarchy table). This is reference material with no algorithmic transformation; all 163 Sub-Industry codes and their hierarchy are implicit inputs to the classification rules.

scoped_out.quote: The Global Industry Classification Standard is designed to be market demand-oriented in its analysis and classification of companies.
scoped_out.concern: Section 1.3 explains the philosophy and design rationale for GICS. No transformation is defined; scoped out as background rationale.

scoped_out.quote: Further information on changes to the GICS Structure can be found on MSCI's website at www.msci.com/gics.
scoped_out.concern: Section 5 describes the GICS Structure Review as a periodic governance and consultation process (internal analysis, public comment, client consultation). No algorithmic transformation is specified for index construction; the outcome of a Structure Review on fixed income classifications is captured in apply_structure_review_to_fixed_income.

scoped_out.quote: The GICS Methodology and Structure fall under the overall supervision of the GICS Operations Committee, with members from both MSCI and S&P Dow Jones Indices.
scoped_out.concern: Section 6 defines organizational governance (Operations Committee membership, confidentiality policy). No transformation.

scoped_out.quote: Section 7 details the GICS Sub-Industry level definitions.
scoped_out.concern: Section 7 provides the enumerated definitions of all 163 GICS Sub-Industries. These definitions are the taxonomy that classification rules reference but are not themselves algorithmic rules. Each definition includes inclusions and exclusions that inform analyst judgement during classification.

scoped_out.quote: The following sections were modified as of March 2023
scoped_out.concern: Appendix 1 is a document changelog listing which sections changed across publication versions. No transformation; scoped out as document metadata.

external_references.item: MSCI GICS methodology set landing page referenced for ongoing changes to the GICS Structure
